question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/service/RecordFileService.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: findByTimestamp(Long timestamp)] Can an unprivileged external user flood the mirror node with rapid sequential calls to findByTimestamp() using a monotonically increasing unique timestamp per request, exhausting the CACHE_MANAGER_RECORD_FILE_TIMESTAMP Caffeine cache (maximumSize=10000) and forcing continuous DB queries, causing sustained database connection pool exhaustion that degrades processing on 30%+ of mirror node instances?
